chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: In today's video, we will learn about Core Web Vitals and why Core Web Vitals are necessary.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: Like this video and keep high energy.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: The thing is that your website should be in your iPhone, iPad, Android, Surface Pro 4 devices.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: Because we have to give a good experience to all the users through our website.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: In this video, we will also learn that Chrome has a hidden tool called Lighthouse and how you can use it to enhance your website.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: So write in the comment section, Sigma Batch OP. Let's go to the computer screen, let's roll the intro.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: So guys, I have come to my computer screen and I will make a new folder called Video 6.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: Video 6 and here in Video 6, I will click on VS Code as always.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: And today I am going to tell you something that is very important in SEO aspect.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: First of all, I want to tell you what SEO is. SEO stands for Search Engine Optimization.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: If you see any website, then SEO is there.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: Let's take an example of Amazon.in.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: If I open Amazon.in, then Amazon.in needs to be loaded as soon as possible so that their products can come in search.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: Google is a search engine. Google's responsibility is to show good pages to users.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: So Google will only want to show those pages which will be fast.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: Information is presented very well and along with that, there are some things that are good for the user.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: For example, there are two pages from the editor. One page has told about dog food.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: The other page has also told exactly about dog food.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: But among these two, page 2 is very fast loaded. If it is loaded in a good way, then Google will give more preference to it.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: Along with that, there are core web vitals which Google considers to rank two pages.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: First of all, content is the most important. If your content is good on the page, then you can rank your website without the core web vitals.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: But if you are competing with a page which has presented almost similar quality content, then your core web vitals will be seen.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: If your core web vitals are good, then you will be given preference.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: The first thing that comes in core web vitals is your CLS.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: Look at this video which I have run on the web.dev website.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: You will see that someone comes to your website and in order to cancel it, he has canceled it from the wrong way.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: This thing on the top has become visible. He has shifted the layout and the order has been completed.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: But if the user wanted to complete the order, he wanted to click on the top.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: In this way, the user experience is very bad.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: As you have seen in this video, this should not happen. That is why our CLS should be at least.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: Now what is the definition of CLS?

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: Cumulative layout shift means how much the page is shifting while loading.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: Assume that you have loaded the page and this is the first thing you get to see.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: This was above and after that, when this load was done, the part of the key term, then it shifted down.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: The layout should not be shifted. If you put vitoride in images, then the layout should not be shifted.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: The next metric we are going to talk about is LCP.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: And LCP stands for Largest Contextual Paint.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: This means that if a user is viewing a page, then the biggest element is there.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: For example, the biggest part of this image is loading for how long?

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: The entire page must be loaded, but the biggest element that is most important is loading for how long?

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: Web.dev or Google should have a good LCP score of 2.5 seconds.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: That is, the biggest element of the page should be loaded in 2.5 seconds.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: After this comes the third metric, FID, which stands for First Input Delay.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: First input delay means that if a user has clicked on an input element, like on the button or on any type of input given on a page,

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: then how long does that input process take?

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: For example, if you clicked on a button, then how long does that button click browser process?

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: And the meaning of Google and Web.dev should start processing your input in less than 100 milliseconds.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: So these are the three core web vitals, which are very important.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: I will tell you for the information that these are the core web vitals, which are updated as well.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: But these three are very important and you should know.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: Now I will show you what the Lighthouse Report is and why it is generated.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: And how these three core web vitals value will be found there.

chunks.text: So I will tell you here.

chunks.text: You will inspect it by right clicking.

chunks.text: And you can see that it is coming to the right side.

chunks.text: Lighthouse basically generates a report for you.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: And it tells you what can be improved in your HTML page.

chunks.text: So all these suggestions are given to you by Lighthouse.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: And if you incorporate these suggestions, then your website will be loaded very well.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: And the user will have a good experience.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: Now you can see here that image elements do not have explicit width and height.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: So you should give width and height.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: The width and height attribute of the image that we read in the last video should be used.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: So that it is reserved in your browser.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: When we set width and height in HTML, then it is reserved in this browser.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: Otherwise if we do not set height and width, then what will happen is that the browser will not tell us how much space to reserve.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: And the longer the CSS load, the lower the page will shift.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: So we do not want our page to shift during loading time.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: If our page starts shifting, then if we click here, then when it shifts from the mistake, then it can happen that we click here.

But if you are competing with a page which has presented almost similar quality content, then your core web vitals will be seen. If your core web vitals are good, then you will be given preference. Cumulative layout shift means how much the page is shifting while loading. Assume that you have loaded the page and this is the first thing you get to see. If you put vitoride in images, then the layout should not be shifted. The next metric we are going to talk about is LCP. And LCP stands for Largest Contextual Paint. This means that if a user is viewing a page, then the biggest element is there. For example, the biggest part of this image is loading for how long? The entire page must be loaded, but the biggest element that is most important is loading for how long? Web.dev or Google should have a good LCP score of 2.5 seconds. That is, the biggest element of the page should be loaded in 2.5 seconds. After this comes the third metric, FID, which stands for First Input Delay. First input delay means that if a user has clicked on an input element, like on the button or on any type of input given on a page, then how long does that input process take? For example, if you clicked on a button, then how long does that button click browser process? And the meaning of Google and Web.dev should start processing your input in less than 100 milliseconds.
